words.note: pf. partner of pisati; use for a completed piece of writing

example.target: Napisala sam pismo baki.
example.gloss: I wrote a letter to my grandmother.

words.note: aspect pair; opisati = describe once/completely, opisivati = repeatedly or in progress; takes accusative

example.target: Možeš li mi opisati svoj stan?
example.gloss: Can you describe your apartment to me?

words.note: za- = fix in writing so you don't forget; zapisati broj/adresu

example.target: Zapisao sam tvoj broj telefona u bilježnicu.
example.gloss: I wrote your phone number down in a notebook.

words.en: to write out; to print out
words.note: iz- = out; ispisati se = withdraw one's enrollment (opposite of upisati se)

example.target: Učiteljica je ispisala nove riječi na ploču.
example.gloss: The teacher wrote the new words out on the board.

words.note: do- = add on to something already written; cf. dopisivati se = to correspond (separate card)

example.target: Dopisao je rečenicu na kraj teksta.
example.gloss: He added a sentence at the end of the text.

words.note: reflexive; s + instrumental: dopisujem se s prijateljem

example.target: Godinama se dopisujem s prijateljicom iz Splita.
example.gloss: I have been corresponding with a friend from Split for years.

words.note: u + accusative: upisati se na tečaj/fakultet; opposite: ispisati se

example.target: Upisala sam se na tečaj hrvatskog jezika.
example.gloss: I enrolled in a Croatian language course.

words.note: pre- = over/across: copy from a source; liječnik prepisuje lijek = the doctor prescribes medicine

example.target: Prepisao je zadaću od kolege iz razreda.
example.gloss: He copied the homework from a classmate.

words.en: to take a look, have a look at
words.note: pf. partner of gledati; pogledati film = watch a film (to the end)

example.target: Pogledaj ovu fotografiju s putovanja!
example.gloss: Take a look at this photo from the trip!

words.note: pre- = go through item by item: a doctor examines a patient, a teacher checks tests; noun pregled

example.target: Liječnik je pregledao pacijenta i prepisao mu lijek.
example.gloss: The doctor examined the patient and prescribed him medicine.

words.note: raz- = look around in all directions: razgledati grad/muzej

example.target: Jučer smo razgledali centar Zagreba.
example.gloss: Yesterday we looked around the centre of Zagreb.

words.note: sudden, unplanned perception; cf. pogledati = look deliberately

example.target: U gužvi sam ugledao staru prijateljicu.
example.gloss: In the crowd I caught sight of an old friend.

words.note: nad- = over (watch from above); no common pf. partner

example.target: Šef nadgleda rad novih radnika.
example.gloss: The boss supervises the work of the new workers.

words.note: izgledaš umorno = you look tired; izgleda da... = it seems that...; noun izgled

example.target: Danas izgledaš jako umorno.
example.gloss: You look very tired today.

words.en: to fix one's gaze on; to fall for someone
words.note: u + accusative: zagledao se u nju = he stared at her / fell for her

example.target: Zagledala se u izlog trgovine.
example.gloss: She fixed her gaze on the shop window.

words.en: to think (of), have a sudden thought
words.note: momentary thought; na + accusative: pomisliti na nekoga

example.target: Kad čujem more, pomislim na djetinjstvo.
example.gloss: When I hear the sea, I think of my childhood.

words.note: invent a story, excuse or device; ne izmišljaj! = don't make things up!; cf. smisliti = think up (a plan)

example.target: Izmislio je priču da ne mora doći.
example.gloss: He made up a story so he wouldn't have to come.

words.note: o + locative: razmišljam o poslu; razmisliti = finish thinking and be ready to decide

example.target: Moram razmisliti o tvojoj ponudi do sutra.
example.gloss: I have to think over your offer by tomorrow.

words.note: zamisli! = imagine!; zamisliti se = become pensive

example.target: Zamisli da živimo na otoku!
example.gloss: Imagine that we live on an island!

words.en: to get (an idea) into one's head, imagine falsely
words.note: pejorative; si = dative reflexive; umislio si je da je najbolji = he thinks he's the best

example.target: Umislio si je da svi govore o njemu.
example.gloss: He got it into his head that everyone talks about him.

words.note: smisliti plan/rješenje; cf. izmisliti = invent, especially something untrue

example.target: Smislili smo dobar plan za vikend.
example.gloss: We thought up a good plan for the weekend.

words.note: impf. predomišljati se = keep hesitating

example.target: Htio je doći, ali se predomislio.
example.gloss: He wanted to come, but he changed his mind.

words.note: pf. of raditi; in everyday Croatian napraviti is more common

example.target: Sve što je obećao, to je i uradio.
example.gloss: Everything he promised, he actually did.

words.note: the everyday pf. partner of raditi/praviti: napraviti grešku/kolač/red

example.target: Napravila sam kolač za rođendan.
example.gloss: I made a cake for the birthday.

words.en: to work off, get (a task) done
words.note: od- = complete an obligation: odraditi smjenu/posao

example.target: Odradio je smjenu i otišao kući.
example.gloss: He worked his shift and went home.

words.note: pre- = transform: prerađivati voće/ribu; preraditi tekst = rework a text

example.target: U toj tvornici prerađuju ribu.
example.gloss: In that factory they process fish.

words.en: to work out in detail, elaborate
words.note: razraditi plan/ideju; impf. razrađivati

example.target: Moramo detaljno razraditi plan puta.
example.gloss: We have to work out the travel plan in detail.

words.en: to process, cover, work on (a topic, data, land)
words.note: obraditi lekciju = cover a lesson; obrađivati podatke/zemlju

example.target: Na satu smo obradili novu lekciju.
example.gloss: In class we covered a new lesson.

words.note: more 'craft/produce' than napraviti: izraditi nakit/dokument

example.target: Ona izrađuje nakit od morskog stakla.
example.gloss: She makes jewellery from sea glass.

words.note: su- = together + raditi; s + instrumental: surađujem s kolegama; noun suradnja

example.target: Naša škola surađuje s fakultetom u Zagrebu.
example.gloss: Our school cooperates with a faculty in Zagreb.

words.note: zovem, zoveš...; zvati na kavu = invite for coffee; pf. partners are prefixed: pozvati, nazvati

example.target: Zovem se Ana, a kako se ti zoveš?
example.gloss: My name is Ana, and what is your name?

words.en: to call (on the phone, repeatedly); to name
words.note: impf. partner of nazvati (already learned); nazivati stvari pravim imenom = call things by their real name

example.target: Naziva me svaki dan poslije posla.
example.gloss: He calls me every day after work.

words.en: to respond (to a call or invitation)
words.note: takes dative: odazvati se pozivu

example.target: Mnogi su se odazvali pozivu za pomoć.
example.gloss: Many responded to the call for help.

words.note: izazvati požar/reakciju = cause; izazvati nekoga = challenge someone; noun izazov

example.target: Oluja je izazvala velike probleme u prometu.
example.gloss: The storm caused big problems in traffic.

words.en: to stop; to step; to fit (into)
words.note: present stanem, staneš (B1 pattern -ati > -anem); sve ne stane u torbu = it all doesn't fit in the bag

example.target: Autobus je stao ispred škole.
example.gloss: The bus stopped in front of the school.

words.note: postao je liječnik; postaje hladno = it is getting cold

example.target: Nakon studija postala je učiteljica.
example.gloss: After her studies she became a teacher.

words.note: ostati kod kuće; ostalo je malo vremena = little time remains; do not confuse with ostaviti = leave something behind

example.target: Večeras ostajem kod kuće jer pada kiša.
example.gloss: Tonight I am staying home because it is raining.

words.note: na + accusative: pristati na prijedlog; its impf. pristajati you already know as 'to suit/fit', same verb, second meaning

example.target: Na kraju je pristala na naš prijedlog.
example.gloss: In the end she agreed to our proposal.

words.note: + infinitive: prestani pričati!; kiša je prestala = the rain stopped

example.target: Kiša je napokon prestala padati.
example.gloss: The rain finally stopped falling.

words.note: nastao je problem = a problem arose; grad je nastao u srednjem vijeku

example.target: Tako je nastala naša najpoznatija pjesma.
example.gloss: That is how our most famous song came into being.

words.en: to pause, stop for a moment
words.note: brief stop, then you continue; cf. stati = stop (fully)

example.target: Zastao je na trenutak i pogledao more.
example.gloss: He paused for a moment and looked at the sea.

words.en: to be absent; to fail to happen
words.note: izostati s nastave = miss class (s + genitive); rezultat nije izostao = the result did not fail to come

example.target: Zbog bolesti je izostao s nastave tri dana.
example.gloss: Because of illness he was absent from class for three days.

words.note: od + genitive: odustati od plana/studija

example.target: Nemoj odustati od svog sna!
example.gloss: Don't give up on your dream!

words.note: s + instrumental; noun sastanak (already known)

example.target: Sastat ćemo se sutra ispred kazališta.
example.gloss: We will meet tomorrow in front of the theatre.

words.note: postoji/postoje = there is/there are; no pf. partner

example.target: Postoji li ovdje neki dobar restoran?
example.gloss: Is there a good restaurant here?

words.note: držati riječ = keep one's word; držati do sebe = have self-respect; base of a large B1 family

example.target: Majka drži dijete za ruku.
example.gloss: The mother holds the child by the hand.

words.note: impf. izdržavati also means 'to support financially' (izdržavati obitelj)

example.target: Ne mogu više izdržati ovu vrućinu.
example.gloss: I can't stand this heat any longer.

words.en: to keep, retain; to hold up (delay)
words.note: zadržati kartu = keep the ticket; zadržati se = stay longer than planned

example.target: Oprostite, zadržao me posao u uredu.
example.gloss: Sorry, work at the office held me up.

words.note: održati sastanak/govor/obećanje; održavati kuću/vezu = maintain; održati se = take place

example.target: Koncert će se održati na glavnom trgu.
example.gloss: The concert will be held on the main square.

words.note: takes accusative: podržati prijedlog/prijatelja; noun podrška

example.target: Obitelj me podržava u svemu.
example.gloss: My family supports me in everything.

words.note: pridržati vrata/torbu; pridržavati se + genitive = abide by (pravila = the rules)

example.target: Možeš li mi pridržati torbu na trenutak?
example.gloss: Can you hold my bag for a moment?

words.note: za- = begin: zaigrati za momčad = start playing for a team

example.target: Napokon je zaigrao za prvu momčad.
example.gloss: He finally started playing for the first team.

words.en: to play out (a match, a role) to the end
words.note: od- = complete: odigrati utakmicu/ulogu

example.target: Momčad je odigrala odličnu utakmicu.
example.gloss: The team played an excellent match.

words.en: to get carried away playing, come alive in play
words.note: raz- + se = fully enter the activity; also of a team finding its rhythm

example.target: Djeca su se razigrala u parku.
example.gloss: The children got carried away playing in the park.

words.note: s + instrumental, often figurative: poigrati se s osjećajima; impf. poigravati se

example.target: Netko se poigrao s mojim stvarima.
example.gloss: Someone has been playing around with my things.

words.en: to outplay; to betray (trust), get around (rules)
words.note: izigrati pravila/povjerenje; impf. izigravati also = to act/pretend (izigravati žrtvu)

example.target: Izigrao je povjerenje svojih kolega.
example.gloss: He betrayed the trust of his colleagues.

words.note: doživjeti uspjeh/nezgodu; doživjeti stotu = live to be a hundred; noun doživljaj

example.target: Na putovanju smo doživjeli pravu avanturu.
example.gloss: On the trip we experienced a real adventure.

words.note: pre- = through: live through something; colloquially also 'to get by'

example.target: Svi su putnici preživjeli nesreću.
example.gloss: All the passengers survived the accident.

words.note: nad- = beyond: live longer than someone

example.target: Baka je nadživjela sve svoje prijateljice.
example.gloss: Grandma outlived all her friends.

words.en: to immerse oneself (in a role or situation)
words.note: u + accusative: uživjeti se u ulogu

example.target: Glumac se potpuno uživio u ulogu.
example.gloss: The actor completely immersed himself in the role.

words.note: the pf. present is used habitually here: grad oživi ljeti = the town comes alive in summer

example.target: Stari grad oživi svakog ljeta.
example.gloss: The old town comes alive every summer.

words.note: u + locative: uživam u glazbi; same root živ- (living)

example.target: Uživamo u šetnji uz more.
example.gloss: We enjoy a walk along the sea.

words.note: dogovoriti se o + locative or za + accusative; noun dogovor

example.target: Dogovorili smo se za sastanak u petak.
example.gloss: We arranged a meeting for Friday.

words.note: nagovoriti koga na + accusative or da + clause

example.target: Prijatelji su me nagovorili da dođem.
example.gloss: My friends talked me into coming.

words.note: takes a direct object (accusative): ogovarati susjede

example.target: Nije lijepo ogovarati kolege s posla.
example.gloss: It's not nice to gossip about colleagues from work.

words.en: to object; to nag, complain (to someone)
words.note: takes dative: stalno mi prigovara = he/she keeps nagging me

example.target: Uvijek mi prigovara zbog nereda u sobi.
example.gloss: She always nags me about the mess in my room.

words.en: to start speaking; to speak up
words.note: dijete je progovorilo = the child said its first words; progovoriti o problemu = finally speak up about it

example.target: Dijete je progovorilo s godinu dana.
example.gloss: The child started speaking at one year old.

words.note: base pair of the -pustiti family; pusti me! = let me go!; pustiti glazbu = put on music

example.target: Pustili su psa da trči po plaži.
example.gloss: They let the dog run on the beach.

words.note: + dative + da: dopustili su mi da ostanem = they allowed me to stay

example.target: Roditelji su joj dopustili da izađe.
example.gloss: Her parents allowed her to go out.

words.en: to drop; to emit; to leave out
words.note: ispustiti čašu = drop a glass; ispustiti riječ = omit a word

example.target: Ispustila je čašu i razbila je.
example.gloss: She dropped the glass and broke it.

words.en: to give in, yield; to ease off
words.note: popustiti pod pritiskom = give in under pressure; bol popušta = the pain is easing

example.target: Na kraju je otac popustio i kupio psa.
example.gloss: In the end the father gave in and bought a dog.

words.en: to miss (a chance, a bus); to let through
words.note: propustiti priliku/vlak; propustiti nekoga naprijed = let someone pass; not the same as otpustiti = fire

example.target: Propustio sam zadnji autobus za grad.
example.gloss: I missed the last bus to town.

words.note: + dative: prepustiti odluku tebi; prepustiti se + dative = surrender oneself to

example.target: Ovu važnu odluku prepuštam tebi.
example.gloss: I leave this important decision to you.

words.note: napustiti posao/grad/obitelj, stronger and more final than otići

example.target: Mnogi mladi napuštaju selo i odlaze u grad.
example.gloss: Many young people leave the village and go to the city.

words.note: base pair of the -staviti family; staviti knjigu na stol = put the book on the table

example.target: Stavi mlijeko u hladnjak, molim te.
example.gloss: Put the milk in the fridge, please.

words.note: predstaviti se = introduce oneself; to predstavlja problem = that constitutes a problem

example.target: Dopustite da vam predstavim svog kolegu.
example.gloss: Allow me to introduce my colleague to you.

words.note: + infinitive or s + instrumental: nastavi čitati!; noun nastava = classes

example.target: Nakon pauze nastavili smo s poslom.
example.gloss: After the break we continued with work.

words.note: ostaviti ključ kod susjeda; ostaviti nekoga = leave a partner; not the same as ostati = stay

example.target: Ostavila sam kišobran u vlaku.
example.gloss: I left my umbrella on the train.

words.note: sastaviti popis/rečenicu/ormar; noun sastav = essay, composition

example.target: Sastavili smo popis za kupovinu.
example.gloss: We put together a shopping list.

words.en: to set (up), place; to pose (a question)
words.note: postaviti pitanje = ask a question; postaviti stol = set the table

example.target: Smijem li vam postaviti jedno pitanje?
example.gloss: May I ask you one question?

words.note: cf. izostati = be absent (yourself); izostaviti = leave something/someone out

example.target: Izostavili su moje ime s popisa.
example.gloss: They left my name off the list.

words.note: pretpostavljam da... = I suppose that...; from pred + postaviti (place before)

example.target: Pretpostavljam da ćeš doći na večeru.
example.gloss: I assume you will come to dinner.

words.note: rastaviti se = get divorced; rastaviti riječ na slogove = split a word into syllables

example.target: Rastavili su stari ormar prije selidbe.
example.gloss: They took the old wardrobe apart before the move.

words.en: to stop (something); (se) to come to a stop
words.note: transitive: zaustaviti auto/taksi; cf. stati = stop (by yourself, intransitive)

example.target: Policajac je zaustavio auto na cesti.
example.gloss: The policeman stopped a car on the road.

words.note: verbal noun in -nje from ponašati se (to behave)

example.target: Njegovo ponašanje na sastanku me iznenadilo.
example.gloss: His behaviour at the meeting surprised me.

words.note: from razmišljati; način razmišljanja = way of thinking

example.target: Nakon dugog razmišljanja prihvatio je posao.
example.gloss: After long reflection he accepted the job.

words.note: -ost nouns are feminine i-declension; instr. sg. odlučnošću

example.target: Njezina odlučnost pomogla joj je u karijeri.
example.gloss: Her determination helped her in her career.

words.note: from sposoban (capable); sposobnost + genitive or za + accusative

example.target: Ima sposobnost brzog učenja jezika.
example.gloss: He has the ability to learn languages quickly.

words.note: from siguran; sa sigurnošću = with certainty

example.target: Sigurnost putnika je najvažnija.
example.gloss: Passenger safety is the most important thing.

words.note: ne- negates the base noun, a productive pattern

example.target: Prije ispita uvijek osjećam nesigurnost.
example.gloss: Before an exam I always feel uncertainty.

words.note: zbog nemogućnosti + genitive = because it was impossible to...

example.target: Zbog nemogućnosti dolaska otkazao je sastanak.
example.gloss: Because he was unable to come, he cancelled the meeting.

words.note: sličnost između + genitive, or s + instrumental

example.target: Vidim veliku sličnost između brata i sestre.
example.gloss: I see a great similarity between the brother and sister.

words.note: abstract; cf. razlika = a specific difference

example.target: Trebamo poštovati različitost među ljudima.
example.gloss: We should respect diversity among people.

words.note: u budućnosti = in the future

example.target: Nitko ne zna što nosi budućnost.
example.gloss: Nobody knows what the future holds.

words.note: from prošli (last, past); u prošlosti = in the past

example.target: Ne treba živjeti u prošlosti.
example.gloss: One shouldn't live in the past.

words.note: from sada (now); the trio prošlost (sadašnjost) budućnost

example.target: Važno je uživati u sadašnjosti.
example.gloss: It is important to enjoy the present.

words.note: from stvaran (real); u stvarnosti = in reality

example.target: Njegovi planovi su daleko od stvarnosti.
example.gloss: His plans are far from reality.

words.note: from susresti (to encounter); also a sports fixture

example.target: Radujem se našem sljedećem susretu.
example.gloss: I am looking forward to our next meeting.

words.note: from susretljiv, literally someone who 'meets you halfway'

example.target: Hvala vam na susretljivosti i pomoći.
example.gloss: Thank you for your helpfulness and assistance.

words.note: from pošten (already known); cf. iskrenost = sincerity

example.target: Cijenim poštenje više od uspjeha.
example.gloss: I value honesty more than success.

words.note: adjective strpljiv (already known); imati strpljenja s + instrumental

example.target: Za rad s djecom treba puno strpljenja.
example.gloss: Working with children takes a lot of patience.

words.note: from hrabar (already known)

example.target: Za tu odluku treba prava hrabrost.
example.gloss: That decision takes real courage.

words.note: imati slabost prema + dative = have a soft spot for

example.target: Slatkiši su moja najveća slabost.
example.gloss: Sweets are my greatest weakness.

words.note: nemam snage (genitive after negation) = I have no strength

example.target: Nemam više snage za trčanje.
example.gloss: I don't have any more strength for running.

words.note: fleeting a: gen. sg. dojma; ostaviti dobar dojam = make a good impression

example.target: Ostavila je odličan dojam na razgovoru za posao.
example.gloss: She made an excellent impression at the job interview.

words.note: from doživjeti; cf. iskustvo = accumulated experience

example.target: Koncert je bio nezaboravan doživljaj.
example.gloss: The concert was an unforgettable experience.

words.note: uspomena na + accusative; kupiti za uspomenu = buy as a souvenir

example.target: Ova fotografija je uspomena s putovanja.
example.gloss: This photo is a keepsake from the trip.

words.note: from sjećati se; sjećanje na + accusative

example.target: Sjećanje na taj dan još je živo.
example.gloss: The memory of that day is still vivid.

words.note: razlika između + genitive; za razliku od + genitive = unlike

example.target: Koja je razlika između ova dva izraza?
example.gloss: What is the difference between these two expressions?

words.note: biti u vezi = be in a relationship; u vezi s + instrumental = regarding; nemam veze s tim = I have nothing to do with that

example.target: Oni su u vezi već tri godine.
example.gloss: They have been in a relationship for three years.

words.note: from prijetiti (to threaten); prijetnja + dative (a threat to something)

example.target: Suša je velika prijetnja poljoprivredi.
example.gloss: Drought is a big threat to agriculture.

words.note: from podržati; pružiti podršku + dative = give support to someone

example.target: Hvala ti na podršci u teškim trenucima.
example.gloss: Thank you for the support in difficult moments.

words.note: i-declension: gen. pomoći, instr. pomoću (B1 grammar point); hitna pomoć = ambulance

example.target: Trebam pomoć oko zadaće iz matematike.
example.gloss: I need help with my maths homework.

words.note: from dogovoriti se; po dogovoru = as agreed

example.target: Ostajemo pri starom dogovoru.
example.gloss: We are sticking to the old arrangement.

words.note: two meanings: izgovor riječi (pronunciation) vs tražiti izgovor (look for an excuse)

example.target: Uvijek nađe izgovor za kašnjenje.
example.gloss: He always finds an excuse for being late.

words.note: održati govor = give a speech; u govoru = in spoken language

example.target: Direktor je održao kratak govor.
example.gloss: The director gave a short speech.

words.note: izraz lica = facial expression; jezični izraz = a (set) phrase

example.target: Taj izraz koristimo samo u govoru.
example.gloss: We use that expression only in speech.

words.note: from izgledati; plural izgledi = prospects, chances

example.target: Ne sudi ljudima po izgledu.
example.gloss: Don't judge people by their appearance.

words.note: radnja filma/romana = the plot; in grammar, verbs express a radnja (action)

example.target: Radnja filma događa se u Dubrovniku.
example.gloss: The film's plot takes place in Dubrovnik.

words.note: from nazvati/nazivati; cf. ime = personal name; naziv ulice/proizvoda

example.target: Ne mogu se sjetiti naziva te knjige.
example.gloss: I can't remember the title of that book.

words.note: from izazvati; biti izazov za + accusative

example.target: Novi posao je velik izazov za mene.
example.gloss: The new job is a big challenge for me.

words.note: from sadržavati (to contain); sadržaj knjige = table of contents

example.target: Sadržaj knjige nalazi se na početku.
example.gloss: The table of contents is at the beginning of the book.

words.note: same root as nastaviti; na nastavi = in class; izostati s nastave = miss class

example.target: Nastava počinje u osam sati.
example.gloss: Classes start at eight o'clock.

words.note: from sastaviti; školski sastav = school essay; sastav momčadi = team line-up

example.target: Za zadaću pišemo sastav o obitelji.
example.gloss: For homework we're writing an essay about family.

words.note: explains manner: naučio je tako što je vježbao = he learned by practising

example.target: Naučila je jezik tako što je puno čitala.
example.gloss: She learned the language by reading a lot.

words.note: način = way, manner; also na koji način? = in what way?

example.target: Na taj način štedimo vrijeme i novac.
example.gloss: In that way we save time and money.

words.note: između + genitive

example.target: Razgovarali smo, između ostalog, o poslu.
example.gloss: We talked, among other things, about work.

words.note: softens a statement

example.target: Na neki način ga razumijem.
example.gloss: In a way I understand him.

words.note: used with present tense for ongoing situations

example.target: U posljednje vrijeme puno radim.
example.gloss: Lately I have been working a lot.

words.note: sve + comparative = increasingly: sve bolje, sve teže

example.target: Sve više ljudi radi od kuće.
example.gloss: More and more people work from home.

words.note: with a noun takes genitive: sve manje vremena

example.target: Imamo sve manje slobodnog vremena.
example.gloss: We have less and less free time.

words.note: expresses gradual change

example.target: Cijene rastu iz dana u dan.
example.gloss: Prices are rising day by day.

words.note: synonym of ponekad (already known)

example.target: S vremena na vrijeme odem u kazalište.
example.gloss: From time to time I go to the theatre.

words.note: prosjek = average; iznad/ispod prosjeka = above/below average

example.target: U prosjeku spavam sedam sati.
example.gloss: On average I sleep seven hours.

words.note: + genitive: na temelju rezultata; temelj = foundation

example.target: Odluku su donijeli na temelju rezultata.
example.gloss: They made the decision on the basis of the results.

words.note: + accusative: s obzirom na kišu; also s obzirom na to da + clause

example.target: S obzirom na kišu, ostajemo kod kuće.
example.gloss: Given the rain, we are staying home.

words.note: + accusative; odnos = relation(ship)

example.target: U odnosu na prošlu godinu, cijene su više.
example.gloss: Compared to last year, prices are higher.

words.note: što + comparative = as ... as possible: što bolje, što više

example.target: Javi mi se što prije, molim te.
example.gloss: Get in touch as soon as possible, please.

words.en: as far as I am concerned
words.note: ticati se + genitive = to concern; to me se ne tiče = that's none of my business

example.target: Što se mene tiče, možemo krenuti odmah.
example.gloss: As far as I'm concerned, we can leave right away.

words.note: hedging phrase; also koliko ja znam

example.target: Koliko znam, trgovina radi do osam.
example.gloss: As far as I know, the shop is open until eight.

words.note: from varati se = to be mistaken

example.target: Ako se ne varam, sutra je praznik.
example.gloss: If I'm not mistaken, tomorrow is a holiday.

words.note: abbreviated itd.

example.target: Kupili smo kruh, sir, voće i tako dalje.
example.gloss: We bought bread, cheese, fruit and so on.

words.note: abbreviated i sl.

example.target: Na tečaju pišemo pisma, sastave i slično.
example.gloss: In the course we write letters, essays and the like.

words.note: also written takoreći

example.target: On je, tako reći, član naše obitelji.
example.gloss: He is, so to speak, a member of our family.

words.note: + genitive: umjesto kave; umjesto da + clause = instead of doing

example.target: Umjesto kave, danas pijem čaj.
example.gloss: Instead of coffee, today I am drinking tea.

words.note: činiti se + dative; čini se da... = it seems that...

example.target: Čini mi se da će padati kiša.
example.gloss: It seems to me that it is going to rain.
